source_description: In a coffee shop, a bald man stands behind the counter. A woman's off-screen voice says, "I'm going to give you my week's notice." The bald man responds, "What?"
Rachel, wearing a green top, stands near the counter and says, "Gunther, I quit."
Rachel, wearing a green top, stands near the counter and says, "Gunther, I quit."
Joey and Chandler sit on a sofa in the coffee shop, their heads turned toward Rachel.
Joey turns his head slightly toward Chandler and asks, "Does this mean we're gonna have to start paying for coffee?"
Joey turns his head slightly toward Chandler and asks, "Does this mean we're gonna have to start paying for coffee?"
Rachel stands by the counter, her shoulders slumping and her gaze lowering. The setting changes to the exterior of the coffee shop at a street corner with snow falling.
Rachel stands by the counter, her shoulders slumping and her gaze lowering. The setting changes to the exterior of the coffee shop at a street corner with snow falling.
Rachel stands by the counter, her shoulders slumping and her gaze lowering. The setting changes to the exterior of the coffee shop at a street corner with snow falling.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Ross sits at a round table writing, while Chandler sits opposite him pressing buttons on an electronic device.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Ross sits at a round table writing, while Chandler sits opposite him pressing buttons on an electronic device.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Ross sits at a round table writing, while Chandler sits opposite him pressing buttons on an electronic device.
The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Ross sits at a round table writing, while Chandler sits opposite him pressing buttons on an electronic device.
Chandler holds up the device to Ross, smiling.
Chandler holds up the device to Ross, smiling.
Ross looks down at the device in Chandler's hands.
Ross looks down at the device in Chandler's hands.
Ross asks, "What?" Chandler continues smiling and says, "I spelled out boobies."
Ross asks, "What?" Chandler continues smiling and says, "I spelled out boobies."
Ross stares at Chandler, his expression remaining neutral. A dark-haired woman, Monica, approaches the table and reaches into a cardboard box on a chair.
Ross stares at Chandler, his expression remaining neutral. A dark-haired woman, Monica, approaches the table and reaches into a cardboard box on a chair.
Ross stares at Chandler, his expression remaining neutral. A dark-haired woman, Monica, approaches the table and reaches into a cardboard box on a chair.
Monica pulls out two colorful boxes. "Ross, put me down for another box of the Mint Treasures," she says. Ross says, "Okay."
Monica pulls out two colorful boxes. "Ross, put me down for another box of the Mint Treasures," she says. Ross says, "Okay."
Monica pulls out two colorful boxes. "Ross, put me down for another box of the Mint Treasures," she says. Ross says, "Okay."
Monica looks at the boxes in her hands, her eyebrows drawing together. "Where are the Mint Treasures?"
Monica holds up the two boxes, revealing they are open and empty. Ross turns to look at her.
Monica holds up the two boxes, revealing they are open and empty. Ross turns to look at her.
Ross says, "We're out. I sold them all." Monica turns her head quickly toward him and asks, "What?"
Ross says, "We're out. I sold them all." Monica turns her head quickly toward him and asks, "What?"
Ross points a pen toward Monica. "Monica, I'm cutting you."
Ross points a pen toward Monica. "Monica, I'm cutting you."
Monica's eyes widen, and her jaw drops slightly.
Monica says softly, "No."
Monica shifts her weight, holding the empty boxes with her mouth slightly open.
Monica shifts her weight, holding the empty boxes with her mouth slightly open.
Monica takes a step closer and says, "Just a couple more boxes," her voice slightly shaky.
Monica lowers the boxes slightly.
Monica waves her hand and smiles faintly. "It's no big deal, right? I'm cool," she says.
Monica waves her hand and smiles faintly. "It's no big deal, right? I'm cool," she says.
Monica continues holding the empty boxes, her breathing slightly heavy.
Monica raises one box slightly and says, "You got to help me out with a couple more boxes," her pitch rising.
Ross raises his hands slightly, looking steadily at Monica.
Ross raises his hands slightly, looking steadily at Monica.
Ross points at her and says, "Mom, look at yourself. You have cookie on your neck."
Monica quickly touches her neck, turns, and walks away from the table. Chandler leans his arms on the table.
Monica quickly touches her neck, turns, and walks away from the table. Chandler leans his arms on the table.
Monica quickly touches her neck, turns, and walks away from the table. Chandler leans his arms on the table.
Chandler asks, "So how many you sold so far?" Ross taps the paper with his pen and says, "Check this out."
Chandler asks, "So how many you sold so far?" Ross taps the paper with his pen and says, "Check this out."
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "517 boxes." Chandler's eyebrows raise, and he says, "Oh, my God."
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "517 boxes." Chandler's eyebrows raise, and he says, "Oh, my God."
Chandler asks, "How did you do that?" Ross replies, "Okay."
Chandler asks, "How did you do that?" Ross replies, "Okay."
Ross gestures with his hands as he says, "The other night I was leaving the museum just as Laser Floyd was letting out of the planetarium."
Ross gestures with his hands as he says, "The other night I was leaving the museum just as Laser Floyd was letting out of the planetarium."
Ross gestures with his hands as he says, "The other night I was leaving the museum just as Laser Floyd was letting out of the planetarium."
Ross continues to gesture.
Ross gestures with one hand. "Without even trying, I sold 50 boxes."
Ross gestures with one hand. "Without even trying, I sold 50 boxes."
Ross raises both hands slightly. "That's when it occurred to me. The key to my success? The munchies."
Ross raises both hands slightly. "That's when it occurred to me. The key to my success? The munchies."
Ross continues gesturing as Chandler smiles.
Ross continues gesturing as Chandler smiles.
Ross points both index fingers forward and says, "So I started hitting the NYU dorms around midnight."
Ross points both index fingers forward and says, "So I started hitting the NYU dorms around midnight."
Ross smiles broadly, maintaining eye contact with Chandler.
Ross smiles broadly, maintaining eye contact with Chandler.
Ross says, "I am selling cookies by the cases," keeping his wide smile.
Ross says, "I am selling cookies by the cases," keeping his wide smile.
Ross points his thumb at his chest and says, "They call me Cookie Dude."
Ross points his thumb at his chest and says, "They call me Cookie Dude."
Chandler stares at Ross without changing his facial expression. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler stares at Ross without changing his facial expression. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Okay, but I'm not going to call you that."
Chandler says, "Okay, but I'm not going to call you that."
Rachel walks quickly through the apartment door, carrying a stack of papers.
Rachel drops the papers on the table. "Okay, stuff that you're doing. I need envelope stuffers. I need stamp lickers," she says.
Rachel drops the papers on the table. "Okay, stuff that you're doing. I need envelope stuffers. I need stamp lickers," she says.
Rachel drops the papers on the table. "Okay, stuff that you're doing. I need envelope stuffers. I need stamp lickers," she says.
Chandler picks up one of the papers. "Well, hey, who did these resumes for you?" Rachel replies, "Me. On my"
Chandler picks up one of the papers. "Well, hey, who did these resumes for you?" Rachel replies, "Me. On my"
Chandler picks up one of the papers. "Well, hey, who did these resumes for you?" Rachel replies, "Me. On my"
Rachel continues, "computer." Chandler looks at the paper and says, "You sure used a large font."
Chandler continues looking at the paper.
Chandler says, "Yeah, well, Waitress at a Coffee House and Cheer Squad Co captain only took up so much room."
Chandler says, "Yeah, well, Waitress at a Coffee House and Cheer Squad Co captain only took up so much room."
Chandler says, "Yeah, well, Waitress at a Coffee House and Cheer Squad Co captain only took up so much room."
Chandler says, "Yeah, well, Waitress at a Coffee House and Cheer Squad Co captain only took up so much room."
Rachel stands nearby with her hands near her hips, looking steadily at Chandler.
Rachel forces a smile and says, "Hey, that's funny."
Rachel's smile drops. She says, "You're funny, Chandler."
Rachel says, "You're a funny guy," her voice tightening.
Rachel raises her hands slightly and asks, "You know what else is really funny? Something else I might have said?"
Rachel raises her hands slightly and asks, "You know what else is really funny? Something else I might have said?"
Rachel raises her hands slightly and asks, "You know what else is really funny? Something else I might have said?"
Chandler stands up from his chair, still holding the paper.
Chandler looks away from Rachel. "I don't know." Rachel points at him. "I don't know."
Chandler looks away from Rachel. "I don't know." Rachel points at him. "I don't know."
Rachel gestures broadly with both arms. "Weren't you the guy that told me to quit my job when I had absolutely nothing else to"
Rachel gestures broadly with both arms. "Weren't you the guy that told me to quit my job when I had absolutely nothing else to"
Rachel stops speaking, her chest heaving as she stares fixedly at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel stops speaking, her chest heaving as she stares fixedly at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross, still seated, gestures with one hand and says softly, "sweetie, calm down. It's gonna be okay."
Ross, still seated, gestures with one hand and says softly, "sweetie, calm down. It's gonna be okay."
Rachel turns toward Ross, her volume loud. "No, it's not gonna be okay, Ross. Tomorrow is my last day, and I don't have a lead."
Rachel turns toward Ross, her volume loud. "No, it's not gonna be okay, Ross. Tomorrow is my last day, and I don't have a lead."
Rachel turns toward Ross, her volume loud. "No, it's not gonna be okay, Ross. Tomorrow is my last day, and I don't have a lead."
Rachel turns away from the table, placing her hands on her hips.
Rachel turns back around. "I'm just going to call Gunther and I'm going to tell him I'm not quitting."
Rachel turns back around. "I'm just going to call Gunther and I'm going to tell him I'm not quitting."
Chandler steps forward holding the paper.
Chandler points at her with the paper in his hand. "You don't want to give in to The Fear."
Rachel points her finger forcefully at Chandler, stepping toward him. "You and your stupid fear. I hate your fear."
Rachel points her finger forcefully at Chandler, stepping toward him. "You and your stupid fear. I hate your fear."
Rachel continues to advance, her volume loud. "I would like to take you and your Fear."
Rachel continues to advance, her volume loud. "I would like to take you and your Fear."
Joey opens the apartment door and walks in, his arms raised slightly. "Hey," he says.
Joey walks further into the room, lowering his arms.
Joey smiles and says, "I got great news." Chandler quickly turns and points at Joey, shouting, "Run, Joey. Run for your life."
Joey smiles and says, "I got great news." Chandler quickly turns and points at Joey, shouting, "Run, Joey. Run for your life."
In an apartment living room, Joey stands facing Rachel.
Rachel touches her forehead and asks, "What?" Joey raises a hand and says, "Rachel, listen."
Joey asks, "Have you ever heard of Fortunata Fashions?" Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
Joey asks, "Have you ever heard of Fortunata Fashions?" Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
Joey asks, "Have you ever heard of Fortunata Fashions?" Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "My old man's doing a plumbing job down there and he heard they have an opening."
Joey gestures with one hand and says, "My old man's doing a plumbing job down there and he heard they have an opening."
He continues, "So you want me to see if he can get you an interview?"
He continues, "So you want me to see if he can get you an interview?"
Rachel steps forward, smiles widely, and hugs Joey, saying, "Oh, my God. Yes. I would love that. Oh, that's so sweet, Joey."
Rachel steps forward, smiles widely, and hugs Joey, saying, "Oh, my God. Yes. I would love that. Oh, that's so sweet, Joey."
Joey pats her back and they separate. He says, "Not a problem. And now for the great"
Joey pats her back and they separate. He says, "Not a problem. And now for the great"
Joey finishes, "news." Rachel asks, "What, that wasn't the great news?"
Joey finishes, "news." Rachel asks, "What, that wasn't the great news?"
Joey holds up an aerosol can and replies, "Only if you think it's better than this snow in a can."
Joey holds up an aerosol can and replies, "Only if you think it's better than this snow in a can."
Joey holds up an aerosol can and replies, "Only if you think it's better than this snow in a can."
Joey looks at the label on the can.
Joey gestures with his free hand, saying, "I got it at work, Mon, you want me to decorate the window? Give it kind of a Christmas looky?"
Joey gestures with his free hand, saying, "I got it at work, Mon, you want me to decorate the window? Give it kind of a Christmas looky?"
Joey gestures with his free hand, saying, "I got it at work, Mon, you want me to decorate the window? Give it kind of a Christmas looky?"
Joey gestures with his free hand, saying, "I got it at work, Mon, you want me to decorate the window? Give it kind of a Christmas looky?"
The setting changes to an exterior nighttime view of a city skyline across a body of water.
The setting changes to an exterior nighttime view of a city skyline across a body of water.
The setting changes to an outdoor Christmas tree lot at night, where Joey and an older man stand among pine trees.
The setting changes to an outdoor Christmas tree lot at night, where Joey and an older man stand among pine trees.
Joey gestures to a tree and says to the man, "Okay, and this one here is a Douglas Fir. Now, it's a little more money, but you get a nicer smell."
Joey gestures to a tree and says to the man, "Okay, and this one here is a Douglas Fir. Now, it's a little more money, but you get a nicer smell."
Joey gestures to a tree and says to the man, "Okay, and this one here is a Douglas Fir. Now, it's a little more money, but you get a nicer smell."
Joey gestures to a tree and says to the man, "Okay, and this one here is a Douglas Fir. Now, it's a little more money, but you get a nicer smell."
The older man nods, examining the tree branches.
The man says, "Looks good. I'll take"
Phoebe interrupts, "no, no, you don't want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one."
Phoebe interrupts, "no, no, you don't want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one."
Phoebe interrupts, "no, no, you don't want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one."
Phoebe interrupts, "no, no, you don't want that one. No, you can have this cool brown one."
The man looks at the brown tree, his facial expression remaining neutral.
The man says, "It's almost dead."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Okay, but that's why you have to buy it so that it can fulfill its Christmas destiny."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Okay, but that's why you have to buy it so that it can fulfill its Christmas destiny."
Phoebe smiles and says, "Okay, but that's why you have to buy it so that it can fulfill its Christmas destiny."
She adds, "Otherwise they're going to throw it into the chipper."
Phoebe turns to Joey and says, "Tell them Joey."
Joey pauses, looking at Phoebe.
Joey nods slightly and says, "Yeah, the trees that don't fulfill their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper."
Joey nods slightly and says, "Yeah, the trees that don't fulfill their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper."
Joey nods slightly and says, "Yeah, the trees that don't fulfill their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper."
Joey nods slightly and says, "Yeah, the trees that don't fulfill their Christmas destiny are thrown in the chipper."
The man shakes his head slightly.
The man says, "I think I'm going to look around a little bit more," and begins to walk away.
Joey turns his head to look at Phoebe.
Joey turns his head to look at Phoebe.
Joey says, "You got to stop doing this. I'm working on commission here."
Joey says, "You got to stop doing this. I'm working on commission here."
Joey says, "You got to stop doing this. I'm working on commission here."
Monica says, "Hey guys. I'm here to pick out my Christmas tree."
Monica says, "Hey guys. I'm here to pick out my Christmas tree."
Phoebe holds out the brown tree and says, "Well, look no further. This one's yours."
Phoebe holds out the brown tree and says, "Well, look no further. This one's yours."
Monica looks at the brown tree.
Monica looks at the brown tree.
Monica points at the tree and asks, "Is this the one that I threw out last year?"
Monica points at the tree and asks, "Is this the one that I threw out last year?"
Phoebe lowers the tree slightly.
Phoebe sighs and says, "All right, you know what?"
Phoebe turns her head away and says, "Never mind. Everybody wants to have a green one."
Phoebe turns her head away and says, "Never mind. Everybody wants to have a green one."
Phoebe brushes the tree's branches and says, "Sorry. I'm sorry. I didn't mean to get so emotional."
Phoebe brushes the tree's branches and says, "Sorry. I'm sorry. I didn't mean to get so emotional."
She continues, "I guess it's just the holidays are just hard."
She continues, "I guess it's just the holidays are just hard."
Monica places a hand on Phoebe's arm and says softly, "Oh honey. Is that because your mom died around Christmas?"
Monica places a hand on Phoebe's arm and says softly, "Oh honey. Is that because your mom died around Christmas?"
Monica places a hand on Phoebe's arm and says softly, "Oh honey. Is that because your mom died around Christmas?"
Phoebe replies, "Oh, I wasn't even thinking about that."
Monica drops her hand from Phoebe's arm as Joey looks on.
Monica drops her hand from Phoebe's arm as Joey looks on.

The setting changes to a room where Ross sits in a circle with several young girls in uniform. A woman in a troop leader uniform stands nearby.

Ross holds a piece of paper and leans toward Charla, the young girl sitting next to him.
Ross holds a piece of paper and leans toward Charla, the young girl sitting next to him.
Ross holds a piece of paper and leans toward Charla, the young girl sitting next to him.
Ross says softly, "How many did you sell?"
Charla looks at him and says, "I'm not going to tell you. You're the bad man who broke Sarah's leg."
Charla looks at him and says, "I'm not going to tell you. You're the bad man who broke Sarah's leg."
Charla looks at him and says, "I'm not going to tell you. You're the bad man who broke Sarah's leg."
Ross leans back slightly.
Ross lowers his voice and says, "Hey, now. That was an accident, okay?"
Charla says, "You're a big scrud."
Charla says, "You're a big scrud."
Ross leans forward again.
Ross asks, "What's a scrud?" Charla retorts, "Why don't you look in the mirror, Scrud?"
Ross asks, "What's a scrud?" Charla retorts, "Why don't you look in the mirror, Scrud?"
